mapping.a_element: hazard/survival curve of token graduation over the 24h observation window (Kaplan-Meier + Cox)
mapping.b_element: emergent mortality curve of organisms over age classes

mapping.a_element: compositional shift toward zero-self-buy tokens driving the cross-regime graduation decline
mapping.b_element: selective disappearance of damage-rich individuals leaving them under-represented in later age classes

brief_justification: The overlap is only the surface vocabulary of survival/hazard/mortality curves: Paper A's transferable machinery is empirical survival-analysis statistics (Kaplan-Meier estimation and Cox proportional-hazards regression fitting covariate hazard ratios to observed graduation events), whereas Paper B's machinery is a mechanistic two-level Moran stochastic process whose scaling limits establish a formal equivalence between multi-level (group) selection and damage-accumulation senescence. Estimating hazards from data is a different mechanism from generating mortality curves via a Moran-type selection dynamic, so no coherent role-for-role machinery mapping holds beyond the shared 'survival curve' outcome.
